name: rsmangler
title: rsmangler

commands.name: rsmangler

arguments.description: show help
arguments.raw: --help, -h: show help

arguments.description: the input file, use - for STDIN
arguments.raw: --file, -f: the input file, use - for STDIN

arguments.description: the output file, use - for STDOUT
arguments.raw: --output, -o: the output file, use - for STDOUT

arguments.description: maximum word length
arguments.raw: --max, -x: maximum word length

arguments.description: minimum word length
arguments.raw: --min, -m: minimum word length

arguments.description: permutate all the words
arguments.raw: --perms, -p: permutate all the words

arguments.description: double each word
arguments.raw: --double, -d: double each word

arguments.description: reverser the word
arguments.raw: --reverse, -r: reverser the word

arguments.description: l33t speak the word
arguments.raw: --leet, -t: l33t speak the word

arguments.description: all posibilities l33t
arguments.raw: --full-leet, -T: all posibilities l33t

arguments.description: capitalise the word
arguments.raw: --capital, -c: capitalise the word

arguments.description: uppercase the word
arguments.raw: --upper, -u: uppercase the word

arguments.description: lowercase the word
arguments.raw: --lower, -l: lowercase the word

arguments.description: swap the case of the word
arguments.raw: --swap, -s: swap the case of the word

arguments.description: add ed to the end of the word
arguments.raw: --ed, -e: add ed to the end of the word

arguments.description: add ing to the end of the word
arguments.raw: --ing, -i: add ing to the end of the word

arguments.description: add common punctuation to the end of the word
arguments.raw: --punctuation: add common punctuation to the end of the word

arguments.description: add all years from 1990 to current year to start and end
arguments.raw: --years, -y: add all years from 1990 to current year to start and end

arguments.description: create an acronym based on all the words entered in order and add to word list
arguments.raw: --acronym, -a: create an acronym based on all the words entered in order and add to word list

arguments.description: add the following words to start and end: admin, sys, pw, pwd
arguments.raw: --common, -C: add the following words to start and end: admin, sys, pw, pwd

arguments.description: add 01 - 09 to the end of the word
arguments.raw: --pna: add 01 - 09 to the end of the word

arguments.description: add 01 - 09 to the beginning of the word
arguments.raw: --pnb: add 01 - 09 to the beginning of the word

arguments.description: add 1 - 123 to the end of the word
arguments.raw: --na: add 1 - 123 to the end of the word

arguments.description: add 1 - 123 to the beginning of the word
arguments.raw: --nb: add 1 - 123 to the beginning of the word

arguments.description: don't check output size
arguments.raw: --force: don't check output size

arguments.description: add spaces between words
arguments.raw: --space: add spaces between words

arguments.description: allow duplicates in the output list
arguments.raw: --allow-duplicates: allow duplicates in the output list

commands.raw_help:
root@kali:~# rsmangler -h
 rsmangler v 1.5 Robin Wood (robin@digi.ninja) <https://digi.ninja>
 
 Basic usage:
 
 	rsmangler --file wordlist.txt
 
 To pass the initial words in on standard in do:
 
 	cat wordlist.txt | rsmangler
 
 To send the output to a file:
 
 	rsmangler --file wordlist.txt --output mangled.txt
 
 	All options are ON by default, these parameters turn them OFF
 
 	Usage: rsmangler [OPTION]
 	--help, -h: show help
 	--file, -f: the input file, use - for STDIN
 	--output, -o: the output file, use - for STDOUT
 	--max, -x: maximum word length
 	--min, -m: minimum word length
 	--perms, -p: permutate all the words
 	--double, -d: double each word
 	--reverse, -r: reverser the word
 	--leet, -t: l33t speak the word
 	--full-leet, -T: all posibilities l33t
 	--capital, -c: capitalise the word
 	--upper, -u: uppercase the word
 	--lower, -l: lowercase the word
 	--swap, -s: swap the case of the word
 	--ed, -e: add ed to the end of the word
 	--ing, -i: add ing to the end of the word
 	--punctuation: add common punctuation to the end of the word
 	--years, -y: add all years from 1990 to current year to start and end
 	--acronym, -a: create an acronym based on all the words entered in order and add to word list
 	--common, -C: add the following words to start and end: admin, sys, pw, pwd
 	--pna: add 01 - 09 to the end of the word
 	--pnb: add 01 - 09 to the beginning of the word
 	--na: add 1 - 123 to the end of the word
 	--nb: add 1 - 123 to the beginning of the word
 	--force: don't check output size
 	--space: add spaces between words
 	--allow-duplicates: allow duplicates in the output list

examples.command:
cat words.txt | rsmangler -m 6 -x 8 --file - > mangled.txt
wc -l mangled.txt
367 mangled.txt
wc -l words.txt
3 words.txt

apt_metadata.description:
Wordlist mangling tool
RSMangler will take a wordlist and perform various
manipulations on it similar to those done by John the
Ripper the main difference being that it will first take
the input words and generate all permutations and the
acronym of the words (in order they appear in the file)
before it applies the rest of the mangles.

use_cases: Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Indexing only — no automated invocation. The operator must manually inspect the repository before adding to a toolchain.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Use as a candidate tool in the chain planner; review provenance before use.
Pair with the LLM system-prompt stanza to ensure the chain planner knows how to call this tool.
